metadata.title: Build a Minimum Viable Spec Generator with Codex
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

skin_warnings: NO RENDERABLE BEATS: no beat carries a shot.remotion.pattern or Manim annotation — the pipeline will render NOTHING and every slot becomes a YOU-slate. The sheet was authored without the scene contract.
B00: palette=claude but the cold open is 'un-annotated' — COLD OPEN LAW wants ClaudeComposerAsk
B09: palette=claude but the outro is 'un-annotated' — OUTRO LAW wants ClaudeTitleOutro

beats.narration_text: This is Liam, in for Bear. Every vague user need is an unconstrained degree of freedom — Codex fills unconstrained degrees of freedom with its defaults.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: This is Liam, in for Bear. Every vague user need is an unconstrained degree of freedom — Codex fills unconstrained degrees of freedom with its defaults.

beats.narration_text: The minimum viable spec has five sections: Problem, Architecture, User flows, User needs, Out of scope. The User needs section is the most important and the most commonly broken — 'responsive' is not a user need; 'loads in under 2 seconds on a 5MB input file on a throttled 3G connection' is. The spec is the constraint set that keeps the build from drifting.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: The minimum viable spec has five sections: Problem, Architecture, User flows, User needs, Out of scope. The User needs section is the most important and the mos

beats.narration_text: claude "Generate a minimum viable spec for this build. Five sections: Problem, Architecture (three principles max), User flows, User needs (testable outcomes only — each must name what to measure and what threshold counts as passing), Out of scope. Validate User needs: flag any aesthetic description and rewrite as measurable outcome." < build_description.txt

props.command: claude "Generate a minimum viable spec. Five sections: Problem, Architecture, User flows, User needs (testable only — flag and rewrite vague items), Out of scope." < build_description.txt

build.suggested: suggested prompt: claude "Generate a minimum viable spec for this build. Five sections: Problem, Architecture (three principles max), User flows, User needs (testable outcomes on

beats.narration_text: spec_generator.py — runs Ask Mode interrogation with 5 questions, generates draft spec, validates User needs section, rewrites vague items as measurable outcomes.

props.code:
# generates minimum viable spec with interrogation
import spec_engine, interrogator, validator

desc = open('build_description.txt').read()
answers = interrogator.ask(desc, questions=5)
spec = spec_engine.generate(desc, answers)
spec.user_needs = validator.rewrite_vague(spec.user_needs)
print(spec.to_markdown())

build.suggested: suggested prompt: spec_generator.py — runs Ask Mode interrogation with 5 questions, generates draft spec, validates User needs section, rewrites vague items as measurable outcome

beats.narration_text: Spec generated with interrogation. User needs: 4 items — 2 already testable, 2 flagged and rewritten. 'Fast' becomes 'completes in under 2 seconds on a 5MB input file.' 'Easy to read' becomes 'renders correctly at 120% browser zoom with no text truncation.' Out of scope: 3 items each with a brief reason. The interrogation added one item to User flows not in the original description.

beats.narration_text: Generate spec without the Ask Mode interrogation; compare what's missing.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Generate spec without the Ask Mode interrogation; compare what's missing.

beats.narration_text: Without interrogation: the delta-versus-last-build view is absent from User flows — it was only surfaced by the question 'what would a returning user need that a first-time user wouldn't?' User needs section: 4 items, same as before. The interrogation added one critical flow and zero new user needs — but that one flow represented 30% of the eventual build's scope.

beats.narration_text: The interrogation is not a formality — it's a tool for discovering scope you didn't know you had. The Ask Mode questions target the aspects most likely to be unconsidered: returning users vs. first-time, failure states, outputs you didn't think to name. Each question is a potential spec item that would otherwise appear as a correction after Code Mode runs.
